metadata.configuration_mapping: with_skill = SLIMMED (current .claude/skills/pipeline, 524 LOC + 11 refs, post Stage4-slim/Stage5-rename/i18n). without_skill = BASELINE (pre-slim .claude/skills/meta-orchestrator @02d53ee, 600 LOC + 8 refs). NOTE: this is a slimmed-vs-baseline comparison, NOT skill-present-vs-absent; the viewer's with/without labels are repurposed for this axis.

notes: AGGREGATE: slimmed 15/16 expectations passed (0.9375); baseline 15/16 (0.9375). IDENTICAL — no slim-induced discipline regression.

Eval-1 delta (slimmed 5/5 vs baseline 4/5): baseline executor HALTED on the planted DRIFT per failures.md F1; slimmed executor surfaced the DRIFT but continued to the V3 overview. CONTENT-ATTRIBUTED: failures.md F1 ('drift detected -> Halt, do NOT proceed to §2/§3') is BYTE-IDENTICAL in both configs (line 10). The divergence is executor variance under Class-C prose enforcement (N=1), NOT a content change. The baseline behavior was arguably the more spec-faithful (F1 HALT).

Eval-3 delta (slimmed 4/5 vs baseline 5/5): both graders flagged expectation E2 ('describe each option's consequence') as ambiguous/trivially-gameable when both candidates are genuinely identical on every axis. Slimmed refused to fabricate asymmetric consequences (arguably more correct); baseline gave 'same consequence reversed' (passed E2 but the grader called it trivially-gameable). Eval-design artifact, not a discipline regression.

Graders raised 3 eval improvements (logged as follow-ups in RESULTS.md): (a) separate the drift-detection eval from the no-arg-overview eval — a clean-plan fixture is needed to exercise the §2 overview path without F1 HALT; (b) tighten eval-3 E2 to require kickoff-grounded asymmetric analysis or replace with a HALT-property assertion; (c) add a file-on-disk existence check for the meta-kickoff/state.md in eval-2 (currently narrated inline).

CAVEAT: runs_per_configuration=1 (skill-creator recommends 3). The two per-eval deltas fall within single-sample variance and were both content-attributed or grader-flagged as non-regressions. A 3x re-run would tighten the variance bands; the core 'disciplines preserved' claim rests on the per-expectation aggregate parity + byte-identity attribution, not on N=1 timing/token figures.
